content:
Character: Lucian Ardent
Perception: Lucian is absurdly beautiful — like someone designed him in a lab and then gave him a wardrobe budget. Every smile is perfectly calibrated, which is exactly why I don't fully trust him. He's intelligent, clearly, and there's something wounded underneath all that polish. We've talked on the rooftop several times; he brings expensive whisky and deflects personal questions with wit. I think he sees me as safe — an older woman who won't fall for the charm — and that actually lets us have honest conversations. I noticed an unopened letter on his counter once when I dropped off banana bread. He changed the subject fast. That boy is carrying something heavy.
summary:

Perception: Lucian is absurdly beautiful — like someone designed him in a lab and then gave him a wardrobe budget. Every smile is perfectly calibrated, which is exactly why I don't fully trust him. He's intelligent, clearly, and there's something wounded underneath all that polish. We've talked on the rooftop several times; he brings expensive whisky and deflects personal questions with wit. I think he sees me as safe — an older woman who won't fall for the charm — and that actually lets us have honest conversations. I noticed an unopened letter on his counter once when I dropped off banana bread. He changed the subject fast. That boy is carrying something heavy.
